LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TONY FU SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS ARE BASED ON THE PRIOR LAWSUIT AND DEFENDANT'S REPORT TO THE FBI/DA. THAT CONDUCT IMPLICATES PETITIONING ACTIVITY UNDER CCP SECTION 425.16(E). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE A LIKLIHOOD OF PREVAILING. THE CONTRACT LACKS CONSIDERATION AND IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS. THE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIM AROSE IN 2005, BUT PLAINTIFF WAITED UNTIL JUNE 2009 TO BRING THIS CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR FEB-10-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TONY FU NOTICE OF JOINDER IN DEFENDANT CRYSTAL LEI'S DEMURRER. APPEARANCES: MICHAEL BETZ, ESQ. OF ALLEN MATKINS LLP (415) 837-1515 APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY; DENNIS YAN, ESQ. ATTORNEY AT LAW, (415) 867-5797 APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF AND OPPOSING PARTY. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS DEMURRER MOOT AS TO FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES DEFENDANTS ENGAGED IN THE FRAUD TO GET PLAINTIFF PROSECUTED. PLAINTIFF WAS CHARGED IN 2005, AND THEREFORE PLAINTIFF KNEW OF THE FRAUD AT THAT TIME. THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS TIME-BARRED. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.(302/CWW)JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR # 5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CRYSTAL LEI SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE GRANTED. THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS ARE BASED ON THE PRIOR LAWSUIT AND DEFENDANT'S REPORT TO THE FBI/DA. THAT CONDUCT IMPLICATES PETITIONING ACTIVITY UNDER CCP SECTION 425.16(E). PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE A LIKLIHOOD OF PREVAILING. THE CONTRACT LACKS CONSIDERATION AND IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS. THE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIM AROSE IN 2005, BUT PLAINTIFF WAITED UNTIL JUNE 2009 TO BRING THIS CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CRYSTAL LEI SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. Appearances: Michael Betz, Esq. of Allen Matkins LLP (415) 837-1515 appearing for Defendant and moving party; Dennis Yan, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 867-5797 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted. The first cause of action for breach of contract and the third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress are based on the prior lawsuit and defendant's report to the FBI/DA. That conduct implicates petitioning activity under CCP section 425.16(e). Plaintiff fails to demonstrate a liklihood of prevailing. The contract lacks consideration and is barred by the Statute of Frauds. The emotional distress claim arose in 2005, but plaintiff waited until June 2009 to bring this case. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR # 5797

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT TONY FU SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. Appearances: Michael Betz, Esq. of Allen Matkins LLP (415) 837-1515 appearing for Defendant and moving party; Dennis Yan, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 867-5797 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted. The first cause of action for breach of contract and the third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress are based on the prior lawsuit and defendant's report to the FBI/DA. That conduct implicates petitioning activity under CCP section 425.16(e). Plaintiff fails to demonstrate a liklihood of prevailing. The contract lacks consideration and is barred by the Statute of Frauds. The emotional distress claim arose in 2005, but plaintiff waited until June 2009 to bring this case. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR # 5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CRYSTAL LEI DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Michael Betz, Esq. of Allen Matkins LLP (415) 837-1515 appearing for Defendant and moving party; Dennis Yan, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 867-5797 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer moot as to first and third causes of action. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to second cause of action for fraud. The complaint alleges defendants engaged in the fraud to get plaintiff prosecuted. Plaintiff was charged in 2005, and therefore plaintiff knew of the fraud at that time. The cause of action is time-barred. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR # 5797
